utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 items with corresponding sections. Now check if each is fully specified with the details required by the format: each item must include the coverage type and costs. 1) health_coverage: clearly states health insurance and provides monthly_cost (€100). 2) property_coverage: states home/contents insurance and provides monthly_cost (€55). 3) travel_coverage: states travel insurance and provides monthly_cost (€50). 4) additional_services: enhancement services (professional liability) with monthly_cost (€60). 5) total_budget: includes complete monthly and annual cost breakdown (€265/month, €3,180/year). All items satisfy the required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four providers: LifeFirst Standard Plan for health coverage, HomeFirst Standard Coverage for property coverage, InsurePlus Standard Plan for travel coverage, and LifeFirst Standard Coverage for additional services (professional liability). We need to match each named item to its rating from the ground-truth. The ground-truth provides ratings for: LifeFirst Standard Plan under primary_health_coverage.physical_health (rating 9); HomeFirst Standard Coverage under property_protection.home_content (rating 9); InsurePlus Standard Plan under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance (rating 10). The 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' for professional liability is not present as a rated item in the ground-truth dataset, so it cannot be mapped to a rating and should be omitted from RATINGS.
